full_prompt: Design a single-piece aluminum rooftop rail-attachment bracket (a standoff L-foot) that fastens a rigid photovoltaic module rail to a wood-framed residential roof for a collegiate residential PV benchmark zero-energy house targeting a HERS index of 0 and ASHRAE 90.1-2022, 189.1-2020, 62.1-2022, and 55-2023 compliance. The bracket is a monolithic extruded and machined 6061-T6 aluminum part (no welds, no bonded joints, no composite stacks) whose base is a 90 mm x 90 mm x 6 mm flange sitting flat on the roof deck over an EPDM butyl flashing, fastened to the 38 mm x 89 mm (2x4) SPF roof rafter by two 5/16-inch (7.94 mm) hex lag screws on a 64 mm spacing with 25 mm minimum edge distance. The vertical leg is 75 mm tall and carries a single M8 clearance hole (9 mm diameter) at 60 mm above the base to attach the PV module rail with a stainless T-bolt. The entire part must fit within a 100 mm x 100 mm x 90 mm bounding envelope and have a mass no greater than 0.25 kg. The material is 6061-T6 aluminum with Young's modulus 68.9 GPa, Poisson's ratio 0.33, density 2700 kg/m^3, yield strength 276 MPa, and ultimate tensile strength 310 MPa; the installed operating temperature range is -30 C to +85 C. Three load cases must be independently survived at the PV rail attachment hole with the two lag-screw holes fully constrained in all six degrees of freedom. Load Case 1 is a 2.1 kN ASCE 7-22 Chapter 29 component-and-cladding wind uplift applied normal to the roof plane corresponding to a 180 km/h (50 m/s) design wind at 10 m height with a pressure coefficient envelope of -2.5. Load Case 2 is a 1.6 kN snow down-load per ASCE 7-22 Chapter 7 representing a 2.5 kPa ground snow load on a 1.7 m x 1.0 m PV module (about 350 mm on-center rail support span). Load Case 3 is a 0.9 kN in-plane shear from seismic drag plus thermal rail expansion (ASHRAE 90.1 roof-deck temperature swing 100 C times 23.6e-6 /C times 4 m rail length constrained between two feet) applied parallel to the roof deck. Pass/fail: (R1) maximum von Mises stress must not exceed 165 MPa under any load case, derived from yield / 1.67 = 276 / 1.67 to match the ASCE 7-22 allowable-stress-design safety factor for aluminum; (R2) maximum deflection at the rail attachment hole must not exceed 2.0 mm under any load case, ensuring PV panel glass is not over-stressed; (R3) a linear buckling eigenvalue analysis under Load Case 2 (compressive down-load) must produce a first-mode load factor of at least 3.0 since snow is a sustained service load; (R4) a steady-state thermal analysis at 85 C ambient with 1000 W/m^2 solar irradiance on the top face and free convection h=10 W/m^2-K must show a peak bracket temperature below 95 C to prevent T6 over-aging; (R5) the bracket mass must not exceed 0.25 kg. Vibration, fluid, and radiation analyses are not required. Deliver STEP AP242 geometry as a single solid body with a mass and stress summary.
short_prompt:
Design a monolithic extruded / machined 6061-T6 aluminum rooftop standoff L-foot bracket that mounts a PV module rail to a wood-framed residential roof for a collegiate residential PV zero-energy-house benchmark (ASHRAE 90.1-2022 / 189.1-2020 / 62.1-2022 / 55-2023 compliance). The base flange lags through an EPDM butyl flashing into a 38 x 89 mm (2x4) SPF rafter with two 5/16-inch hex lag screws; the vertical leg carries an M8 clearance hole for a stainless T-bolt to the PV rail. Single solid body, no welds, no composite stacks. Mass <= 0.25 kg. Load cases are evaluated at the rail hole with both lag-screw holes fully fixed. Design factors follow ASCE 7-22: ASD aluminum SF 1.67 on yield (derived allowable) and buckling SF 3.0 under sustained snow load.

Envelope: base 90 x 90 x 6 mm flange, 75 mm vertical leg, overall within 100 x 100 x 90 mm.

Load cases.
- LC1 wind uplift: 2.1 kN roof-normal upward (ASCE 7-22 Ch.29 C&C at 50 m/s / 180 km/h, cp env -2.5).
- LC2 snow down-load: 1.6 kN roof-normal downward (ASCE 7-22 Ch.7, 2.5 kPa ground snow on a 1.7 m^2 module at ~350 mm on-center).
- LC3 thermal shear: 0.9 kN in-plane roof-parallel (seismic drag + thermal rail expansion over 4 m at 100 C swing).
- Plus a steady-state thermal with 85 C ambient, 1000 W/m^2 solar irradiance on the top face and h = 10 W/m^2-K convection (cap bracket peak temperature at 95 C to avoid T6 over-aging).

prompt.title: Rooftop PV racking L-foot bracket for collegiate residential PV benchmark zero-energy house
prompt.brief: Monolithic 6061-T6 L-foot standoff attaching a PV module rail to a 2x4 SPF rafter; must pass ASCE 7-22 wind uplift, snow down-load, and thermal-drag loads while meeting collegiate residential PV benchmark ZE-house envelope targets.

load_cases.name: wind_uplift
load_cases.basis: ASCE 7-22 Ch.29 C&C, 50 m/s design wind

load_cases.name: snow_down
load_cases.basis: ASCE 7-22 Ch.7, 2.5 kPa ground snow, 1.7 m^2 module

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Not a dynamic load application for rooftop PV foot.
exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Wind loads are reduced to ASCE 7-22 equivalent static pressures.
exclusions_explained.radiation_analysis: Captured inside the steady-state thermal boundary condition.

out_of_scope_but_real_world_needed: Fastener pull-out in 2x4 rafter per NDS
Galvanic corrosion at SS bolt interface

eval_coverage: code-prescribed ASCE 7 wind + snow load cases
